label: Reward modeling

node.label: Reward modeling
node.definition: Human or synthetic comparisons are converted into a learned scalar estimate of desirability.
node.why_it_matters: The role of Reward modeling is to connect Preference learning, Supervised learning to downstream capabilities including Preference optimization (RLHF, DPO families), Reward-model training, RLHF & RLAIF, Reflection, critique, verifier & repair loops.
examples: substrate for Preference optimization (RLHF, DPO families)
substrate for Reward-model training, RLHF & RLAIF
substrate for Reflection, critique, verifier & repair loops
limitations: A tractable objective is a surrogate, so optimizing it can improve the score while leaving the intended behavior unchanged or worse. Its direct dependencies include Preference learning, Supervised learning.

dependencies.rationale: Reward modeling uses Preference learning as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
Reward modeling uses Supervised learning as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
Reward modeling is learned, selected, or evaluated using the objective family Margin, ranking & metric objectives.

sentence_units.text: Human or synthetic comparisons are converted into a learned scalar estimate of desirability.

The role of Reward modeling is to connect Preference learning, Supervised learning to downstream capabilities including Preference optimization (RLHF, DPO families), Reward-model training, RLHF & RLAIF, Reflection, critique, verifier & repair loops.

A tractable objective is a surrogate, so optimizing it can improve the score while leaving the intended behavior unchanged or worse. Its direct dependencies include Preference learning, Supervised learning.
